etymology.description: 創業(創業) combines 創(創) (chuàng, 'create') and 業(業) (yè, 'business'), literally forming 'create business'. 公司(公司) combines 公(公) (gōng, 'public; common') and 司(司) (sī, 'company; manage'), meaning 'corporation' or 'company'. Together, it refers to a 'startup', emphasizing the act of creating a new business.
etymology.interpretation: 創(創) (chuàng, 'create') suggests initiating something new, while 業(業) (yè, 'business') reflects enterprise or undertaking, directly leading to 'entrepreneurship'. The marriage of these concepts underscores the action of founding a business. 公(公) (gōng, 'public; common') with 司(司) (sī, 'company; manage') indicates a structured entity concerned with economic activities, thus forming a 'company'. The complete phrase represents a 'startup', a company at its inception stage focusing on innovative and new ventures.

examples.chinese: 这家创业公司专注于人工智能领域(這家創業公司專注於人工智能領域)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā chuàngyè gōngsī zhuānzhù yú réngōng zhìnéng lǐngyù.
examples.english: This startup focuses on the field of artificial intelligence.

examples.chinese: 他决定加入一个创业公司(他決定加入一個創業公司)，以积累更多经验(以積累更多經驗)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juédìng jiārù yīgè chuàngyè gōngsī, yǐ jīlěi gèng duō jīngyàn.
examples.english: He decided to join a startup to gain more experience.

examples.chinese: 许多创业公司都在快速增长(許多創業公司都在快速增長)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō chuàngyè gōngsī dōu zài kuàisù zēngzhǎng.
examples.english: Many startups are experiencing rapid growth.
